skill.name: longbridge-market-data
skill.description: Use Longbridge market data from the local CLI or MCP fallback for quotes, K-line charts, depth, trades, capital flow, market status, exchange rates, IPO data, ADR premium, and FX carry analysis.
skill.summary: Analyze Longbridge quotes, charts, market status, flows, IPOs, FX data, and premium frameworks.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick and weak-cryptography patterns, but inspected evidence shows markdown inline code, CLI examples, ticker text, and financial formulas rather than executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The confirmed risk is that the skill instructs agents to run the local Longbridge CLI and can use network-backed Longbridge data, so publication is reasonable with an external-command warning.

medium_findings.title: Local Longbridge CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs the agent to run local longbridge commands and check command help. This is expected for the skill, but it gives the agent external command capability and may use an authenticated local session for some functions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected files directly tell the agent to invoke the longbridge CLI. The commands are legitimate product commands, so the concern is operational command execution rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Longbridge Terminal Install Link
low_findings.description: The skill links to the Longbridge terminal GitHub project when the CLI is missing. This is a fixed vendor link and not evidence of exfiltration or arbitrary network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only inspected hardcoded URL is an installation link for the documented CLI. No evidence found that secrets or user data are sent to that URL.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high weak-cryptography alerts correspond to markdown content, financial terms, formulas, or command examples. No cryptographic API, hash function, or password handling code was found in the inspected evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text and CLI help examples, not executable cryptographic code. The pattern names appear triggered by substrings and financial notation.

low_findings.title: Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The many Ruby backtick findings are markdown inline code spans and fenced shell examples. They document Longbridge command usage and are not Ruby source files or executable backtick expressions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are markdown documentation and shell snippets. No Ruby file, Ruby interpreter call, or dynamic shell evaluation was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells agents to run the longbridge CLI, including help commands and market-data commands. Users should only enable it where local command execution is acceptable.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command execution guidance is explicit and central to the skill. The commands are bounded to Longbridge examples, which lowers but does not remove risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Local Session May Be Used
dangerous_patterns.description: Some documented features require a local Longbridge login or active session token. This can expose account-scoped market or IPO data if the user grants access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation states that subscriptions use an OAuth and WebSocket session, and IPO order views require login. No evidence found of credential theft or unsafe token handling.

content.value_statement: Market data questions often require several commands and correct symbol formats. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge quotes, charts, depth, flows, calendars, IPOs, and FX data.
actual_capabilities: Normalizes HK, US, A-share, and Singapore stock symbols before data lookup.
Guides quote, static data, valuation index, K-line, intraday, depth, and trade tick requests.
Supports market status, trading calendars, market temperature, security lists, and participant lookups.
Covers capital flow, trade statistics, A/H premium, ADR premium, and FX carry analysis workflows.
Uses Longbridge CLI JSON output when available and describes MCP fallback paths where documented.
Provides multilingual response guidance for Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English.
limitations: It depends on the local Longbridge CLI or configured Longbridge MCP tools.
Some account-specific commands require the user to authenticate with Longbridge.
Capital flow and several intraday views are limited to current-day data.
It does not provide investment advice or guarantee real-time market availability.

use_cases.title: Answer Stock Quote Requests
use_cases.description: Get a clear price, volume, valuation, and market status summary for one or more supported securities.

use_cases.title: Review Market Microstructure
use_cases.description: Summarize order book depth, recent trades, broker queues, and capital flow using Longbridge data.

use_cases.title: Prepare Cross-Market Briefs
use_cases.description: Compare A/H premiums, ADR premiums, exchange rates, market temperature, IPO calendars, and FX carry inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Check A Quote
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the latest Longbridge quote for NVDA.US? Include price, change, volume, and market status.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Several Symbols
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare 700.HK, AAPL.US, and 600519.SH using Longbridge. Summarize quote, valuation, and trading status.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Intraday Activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge to review 700.HK depth, recent trades, intraday chart, and capital flow. Highlight buy or sell pressure.

prompt_templates.title: Build Cross-Market Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Longbridge brief on A/H premium, ADR premium, FX rates, IPO calendar, and market temperature for today.

output_examples.output: Tencent maps to 700.HK. The response summarizes latest price, daily change, turnover, trading status, and cites Longbridge Securities.

output_examples.input: Show me the order book and trades for 700.HK.
output_examples.output: The response provides bid and ask levels, recent trade direction, any off-hours warning, and a concise pressure summary.

output_examples.input: Is the Hong Kong market open and what is the market temperature?
output_examples.output: The response checks the HK trading session, explains open or closed status, and describes the current sentiment temperature.

best_practices: Confirm ambiguous symbols before running Longbridge commands.
Use JSON output and summarize results in the user requested language.
State data source, market hours, and stale or missing data conditions clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not guess the exchange when a symbol or company name is ambiguous.
Do not present Longbridge data as financial advice or a trade recommendation.
Do not run account-specific commands unless the user requested that account view.

faq.question: Does this skill require Longbridge login?
faq.answer: Most market data workflows are public. Subscriptions, IPO orders, and profit-loss views can require local Longbridge authentication.

faq.question: Which markets are supported?
faq.answer: The skill documents workflows for Hong Kong, US, A-share, and Singapore market data through Longbridge.

faq.question: Can it handle Chinese stock names?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for common Chinese and English names, but it asks when the market is ambiguous.

faq.question: Can it fetch historical candles?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides K-line and historical range requests with supported periods and JSON output.

faq.question: Does it support options or warrants?
faq.answer: No. The K-line guidance says options and warrants should use a derivatives-focused Longbridge skill.

faq.question: What security warning should users know?
faq.answer: The skill asks agents to run the local longbridge command. Enable it only where local command execution is acceptable.
